title: Sed injection in organs/hand.sh edit command

test_cases.name: Edit with safe literal string
test_cases.description: Verify basic edit functionality works with simple alphanumeric strings
test_cases.precondition: Test file created with known content
test_cases.expected_result: File successfully edited, OLD replaced with NEW, no sed errors

test_cases.name: Edit with pipe character (sed delimiter)
test_cases.description: Verify sed pipe delimiter in OLD or NEW is properly escaped
test_cases.precondition: Test file with pipe delimiter content
test_cases.expected_result: Pipe characters properly escaped, file shows literal pipe in content

test_data.injection_risk: Unescaped | would terminate sed s command and start new command

test_cases.name: Edit with ampersand character (sed backreference)
test_cases.description: Verify sed ampersand backreference in replacement is properly escaped
steps: Verify file contains 'check & confirm' exactly (not expanded backreference)
test_cases.expected_result: Ampersand treated as literal character, not as sed backreference

test_data.injection_risk: Unescaped & in replacement expands to matched text, causing duplication

test_cases.name: Edit with regex metacharacters (. * [ ] ^ $)
test_cases.description: Verify regex metacharacters in OLD pattern are properly escaped
steps: Verify file contains 'x.y c*d' with dots as literals
test_cases.expected_result: Dots treated as literal dots, not as regex wildcard

test_data.injection_risk: Unescaped . matches any character; * repeats previous; could match unintended text

test_cases.name: Edit with backslash character
test_cases.description: Verify backslash characters are properly escaped (escape char in sed)
steps: Verify file contains 'newpath\to\file' with backslashes literal

test_data.injection_risk: Backslash is escape char in sed; improper escaping breaks sed syntax

test_cases.name: Edit with forward slash character
test_cases.description: Verify forward slash (alternate sed delimiter context) is properly escaped
test_cases.precondition: Test file with forward slash content
test_cases.expected_result: Forward slashes properly escaped despite using pipe as sed delimiter

test_cases.name: Edit with complex sed injection attempt
test_cases.description: Verify defense against crafted sed injection payload
steps: Verify file contains 'safe', no arbitrary commands executed
test_cases.expected_result: Malicious payload treated as literal string; file unchanged except intended edit

test_cases.name: Edit with square brackets (character class)
test_cases.description: Verify square brackets in pattern are properly escaped
steps: Verify file contains 'test [0-9]' with brackets as literal
test_cases.expected_result: Square brackets treated as literal characters, not as regex character class

test_cases.name: Edit with caret and dollar (anchors)
test_cases.description: Verify line anchors ^ and $ are properly escaped in pattern
test_cases.precondition: Test file with caret and dollar content
steps: Verify file contains 'price = $10 ^cold' with $ and ^ as literals
test_cases.expected_result: Anchors treated as literal characters, not as regex anchors

test_cases.name: Edit with combined special characters
test_cases.description: Verify multiple special characters together are escaped correctly
test_cases.precondition: Test file with mixed special characters
steps: Verify file shows '/home/[user]*' with all specials escaped
test_cases.expected_result: All special characters properly escaped in combination

test_cases.name: Edit with empty string replacement
test_cases.description: Verify edit can delete content by replacing with empty string
steps: Verify file is empty or contains only whitespace

test_cases.name: Edit with newline in content
test_cases.description: Verify edit handles content with actual newline characters
steps: Execute: hand.sh edit targeting single line
Verify only targeted line is replaced
test_cases.expected_result: Single line properly replaced, other lines unaffected

test_cases.name: Edit with global flag (multiple replacements)
test_cases.description: Verify edit replaces all occurrences (g flag)
steps: Create test file: echo 'foo foo foo' > /tmp/test_hand_013.txt
Verify file contains 'bar bar bar' (all 3 replaced)

test_cases.name: Edit backup creation
test_cases.description: Verify backup file is created before editing
test_cases.expected_result: Backup file created with timestamp suffix before editing

test_cases.name: Edit non-existent file error handling
test_cases.description: Verify appropriate error when editing non-existent file
steps: Check exit code and error message
test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed, exit code non-zero, no file created

acceptance_criteria.description: Escape functions properly handle pipe delimiter
acceptance_criteria.validation: TC-002 passes: pipe characters in OLD/NEW are escaped

acceptance_criteria.description: Escape functions properly handle ampersand backreference
acceptance_criteria.validation: TC-003 passes: & in replacement is escaped and not expanded

acceptance_criteria.description: Escape functions properly handle regex metacharacters

acceptance_criteria.validation: TC-007 passes: malicious payloads treated as literal strings
TC-005 passes: escaping backslash first prevents re-escaping

acceptance_criteria.description: Combined special characters are handled safely
acceptance_criteria.validation: TC-010 passes: multiple specials in one string escaped correctly
TC-001,011,012,013 pass: normal edits work as expected
TC-014 passes: backup file created before edit
TC-015 passes: non-existent file error correctly reported

regression_tests.name: Verify other hand.sh commands unaffected
regression_tests.description: Regression test: ensure create, append, delete, copy, call, execute still work
validation_steps: Verify both files exist and contain expected content

regression_tests.name: Verify integration with mouth.sh
regression_tests.description: Regression test: mouth.sh can call hand.sh without breakage

regression_tests.name: Verify performance not degraded
regression_tests.description: Regression test: multiple sequential edits complete in reasonable time

regression_tests.name: Verify large file handling
regression_tests.description: Regression test: edit works on large files without memory issues
validation_steps: Verify completion and no out-of-memory errors

regression_tests.name: Verify message bus integration
regression_tests.description: Regression test: hand.sh log_action calls still work
validation_steps: Verify log entry contains: HAND_EDIT, filename, old→new

notes.escape_pattern_logic: escape_sed_pattern escapes . * [ ] ^ $ | \ in order, backslash first to avoid re-escaping
notes.sed_delimiter: Using | as delimiter instead of /, safe for paths with /
notes.test_environment: Bash on Linux, /tmp for test files, cleanup after each test
notes.test_data_sensitivity: No sensitive data; all test data is arbitrary strings
notes.automation_notes: Tests can be automated in CI/CD pipeline after manual verification
